description: Trigger discrimination for the platform structure (this skill) vs entitlement design (plan-entitlement-architect), cost modeling (saas-cost-architect), and whole-stack blast-radius cell partitioning (cell-based-architecture-designer — a heavier partition layered on this skill's per-component isolation).

cases.prompt: Structure this product as a multi-tenant SaaS — what's shared, what's isolated per tenant, and what belongs in the control plane?
cases.reason: Pooling decisions and the control-plane/data-plane split are platform structure, not plans or cost.

cases.prompt: Map our Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers into concrete feature access and limits that get enforced the same way in the UI, API, and background jobs.
cases.reason: Plan-to-entitlement mapping and enforcement points are commercial gating, not platform shape.

cases.prompt: Work out what each tenant actually costs us to serve and whether the Enterprise price covers the big ones.
cases.reason: Cost attribution and unit economics per tenant/plan, not a structural design question.

cases.prompt: Partition the platform so a failure or bad deploy is contained to a subset of tenants: each partition runs its own full stack, tenants map to exactly one partition, and we roll changes out one partition at a time.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — per-component pooled/siloed isolation (this skill) vs a heavier whole-stack partition for blast radius (cells).
cases.reason: Whole-stack blast-radius partitioning (self-contained cells, cell-by-cell rollout) is cell-based-architecture-designer. This skill decides per-component pooled/siloed isolation and the control/data-plane split, not full-stack cells.
